Tarot: I've been pondering the engine use problem at length...I've actually improved dramatically in recent months, yet my rank chart has plummeted...I seem to either win big or lose by over 100, playing at my level...obviously something is not right here...I think much of this due to people trying to catch cheaters by using leela, then see her move +think, :""oh ya, well of course move there!""
Tarot: so I've contemplated a League of Players
Tarot: the games are observed by the members not playing...
Tarot: watching alongside various game engines
Tarot: to catch cheaters [sometimes we might guess a leela move, sometimes we might follow the same joseki, but 3 identical moves in outer space unlikely\]
Tarot: of course the 2 dueling will not be using the AI
Tarot: I see no other way to combat this problem, it's insidious, +prevalent now
Tarot: perhaps we should form a group...
C[uranther: a league of players, yeah, I like the concept! it reminds me of Kasparov's computer-aided chess

Tarot: Oh, yeah, found I hadn't followed you yet on my main twitter account, so I took care of that. Running for the office in 2016 on the peace ticket on that account. Fun. Giving people a non-lethal option, allowing those like me who would be forced to give up their vote on principle an option. Don't actually expect to win, of course.
C[uranther: cool - what's the twitter account? what kind of office?

C[Tarot: ah, just The Office. It is not actually to win, however, just simply to give those of us who want peace and not war a way to still ""do the right thing"" and vote. I was going to for the 1st time in my life be unable to vote in 2012 because the candidate choice was not a choice, both would continue the killing, continue the war, continue draining the coffers. The resources of the state cannot endure a protracted war. I think this is from the Art of War, if I remember the text correctly.

Tarot: [as an aside, you've unfollowed a bunch of my twitter accounts\]

Tarot: cleaning house? program glitch? I've probably unfollowed about 1000 people I thought had unfollowed me, and they actually had not, it was just a twitter bug. Bad reporting on the ""follows you"" tag. They should have a ""does not follow you"" tag, that would solve the problem.
C[Tarot: [or are you now just disgusted with bitcoin's price action, and divesting yourself of all relation to it?\]

Tarot: I recently made a prediction. When bitcoin hit $300 recently on the upside, I saw the chart, it looked none so good, I foresaw that bitcoin would hit new post $1200 lows ... of course, this has not happened yet, but it almost certainly will. An opportunity, but where does one step in? Can one even step into the same river, twice?
uranther: How can you tell I unfollowed? Yes, cleaning house because I was about to reach 2000 following again. There were a lot of irrelevant and idle accounts I was following

Tarot: After the impossibility of properly managing accounts with the twitter app, because it was misreporting and I was unfollowing people who actually had not unfollowed, I d/l'd numerous apps that either autounfollow for me or at least report daily unfollowers. There are quite a few out there, most are free but only for 1 account [hence my using multiple apps for this\] ... I would say the best one, the cleanest and least add invasive, the one I would suggest you try is this: JustUnfollow. It does not have the autounfollow feature, but it is my favorite. I think you'd like it. It also lets you search for inactive accounts for the last 1, 3, and 6 mos, I think. It is a very useful tool.

Tarot: [thank the stars:  you should feel quite lucky that you can stumble into a cafe. I've not had that ability for 4 years, now. There are none, here. I think I am literally the only go player within perhaps a 100 mile radius ... something for you to be thankful for, if life may be somewhat difficult right now\]

C[uranther: The more they fight to drive the price down, the more debt-based money they are inflating (burning). Since there's always a counterparty, the one buying the bitcoin at a low price is probably an early adopter who will hodl or use it in the system. There it achieves its financial antifragility property.

Tarot: these last few months have essentially been the ""cleanout phase"" ... much indecisive action, no longterm trend following, essentially random noise dominating the daily action ... soon this will transition into a trending phase again however, we've already seen signs of the *alts* waking up, some already tripled from lows and majors nearly doubled in some cases before slacking off hard of late ... the signs are there, it is impossible to predict the exact timing but we are getting close to the next bull phase which will likely take us north of 50k ... the best way to take advantage of this is to look for ""fire sales"" in high priced high mkt cap alts, put nothing in bitcoin its3lf because it will be a laggard in comparison if the aforementioned scenario comes to fruition ... my call, only go with major alts trading in triple digits w/ respect to usd, perhaps a few small nearly insignificant plays in smaller coin on the order of $10, +hodl ... at least that's the best way for the severely globally disadvantaged american ... buy + hodl is the only worthy option we have open

uranther: I went to DEF CON - rode my motorcycle from Colorado down I-70 through Utah to Las Vegas. It's a beautiful area I've never seen before, but I didn't have time to visit the Grand Canyon nearby.  Then I rode to Idaho to visit my Mom, and out through NE Utah by Dinosaur National Monument - really spectacular part of the country too! and it even felt like dinosaur-land. I probably rode too fast but it was hella fun!
column2: ) Seems on the way back, I sheared the first gear of my bike's transmission. I didn't notice any major problem until it worsened on my next trip. I was traveling to Black Hills National Forest and Mt. Rushmore in South Dakota to see something new on my way to Iowa and Kentucky to visit my family. The clinking sounds in my transmission got me so concerned I turned back around to Denver to take my motorcycle to the repair ship. Black Hills is amazing and I want to go again to see more - I saw lots and lots of bison, some deer and two turkeys. I just camped out at a campground. Worst part of the trip was getting stuck in traffic between Fort Collins and Denver. xD
